behaviors.description: The generated function signature matches the expected interface (parameter names, types, return type) specified in the description.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the model’s semantic mapping from NL spec to code signature. A stubbed model would hide this. Rejected Workflow: behavior only requires a single generation decision, not multi-step agent chaining.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real LLM with interface-spec prompts, parse generated signature, and assert exact match on params/types/return type across repeated runs to catch model-output failures.

behaviors.description: Orchestration executes attempts in order and returns immediately on the first passing attempt.
behaviors.justification: This is module handoff/orchestration logic (attempt loop + runner result + stop condition). In-process stubs are sufficient. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow: behavior text is about ordering/early-return control flow, not real OS/network/subprocess behavior or model-output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator, attempt store, and stubbed generator/test-runner modules; force fail then pass; assert attempt order and immediate stop after first pass.

behaviors.description: For a function that a single call usually fails, the live iterative loop can still produce a passing implementation within the allowed attempts.
behaviors.justification: Requires multiple real agentic steps (generate -> test feedback -> refine -> retest) and success depends on live model behavior over iterations. Mocking would miss the real failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Run full live loop against a benchmark function known to often fail first attempt; require pass within <=3 attempts and record per-attempt outcomes.

behaviors.description: When tests fail, the refinement prompt builder includes the failed test names and failure messages in the next LLM prompt.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at boundary between test-result objects and prompt-construction/orchestration modules. Deterministic fixtures catch missing/incorrect propagation. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: behavior checks prompt content inclusion, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Feed real failed-test artifacts from runner module into refinement prompt builder via orchestrator path; assert next prompt contains exact failed test names and failure messages.

behaviors.description: The next live attempt changes implementation content in response to prior failure context rather than resubmitting the same code.
behaviors.justification: Needs at least two sequential model decisions with context carryover; correctness depends on real LLM reacting to prior failure info. A stub can trivially return different code and hide true behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run live attempt 1 to failure, pass real failure context into attempt 2 prompt, and assert attempt 2 code is materially changed and aligned with the failing-test context.

behaviors.description: The service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic loop-limit + bookkeeping + error-shaping across local modules. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow: behavior text is purely control/state/output contract and does not require real OS/network/model-quality decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Use stub generator and stub runner that always fail; execute loop; assert exactly 3 attempts and structured error payload includes each attempt’s test results.

behaviors.description: The full non-agentic pipeline wiring (generate call, test execution, attempt bookkeeping) works end to end with deterministic stubbed model outputs.
behaviors.justification: "Full ... end to end" plus real test execution commonly crosses subprocess/OS boundaries; fake runners can hide path, exit-code, and process-invocation failures. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: behavior explicitly says non-agentic and deterministic stubbed model outputs.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service end-to-end with deterministic model stubs but real test execution path (actual subprocess/runner invocation), and verify generate->execute->bookkeeping flow and outputs.

behaviors.description: The full live loop (generate, test, refine, retest) solves at least one known hard function that fails in the first attempt.
behaviors.justification: Explicitly a full live multi-step agent pipeline with sequential model decisions and feedback-driven refinement. Lower levels cannot validate this emergent loop behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full live pipeline on a curated hard task where first attempt is expected to fail; require eventual pass after refinement within attempt budget.
